article_meta.title: Why a nation of 1.45 billion wants more children16 December 2024ShareSaveSoutik BiswasIndia correspondentShareSaveAFPLas

reasoning: The model commits two attribution rule violations and has one defensible finding. Item 1 flags Mohan Bhagwat's quoted text ('According to population science...') — this is clearly inside quotation marks and attributed to a named source. Item 2 flags Tim Dyson's quote ('socially, politically and economically disastrous') — this is also quoted language attributed to Mr Dyson. Only Item 3 ('aggressive family welfare programmes') is genuinely the author's own prose and is a defensible Word Choice flag. The model also misses several author-level bias instances: the informal/editorializing tone ('But guess what?'), 'nudged past China' (word choice), 'more exaggerated, noises' (subjective qualifying adjective), 'As if the southern states' concerns weren't enough' (editorializing), and the characterization of RSS as 'Hindu nationalist' with 'ideological backbone' framing.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Two out of three flagged items are direct quotes from named sources (Mohan Bhagwat and Tim Dyson). These are clear attribution rule violations — the text is inside quotation marks and explicitly attributed.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Two of the three items are attribution violations and therefore false positives. Only 'aggressive family welfare programmes' is a genuine author-level bias instance. That's a 2/3 false positive rate.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed several clear author-prose bias instances: 'But guess what?' (editorializing/Spin), 'nudged past' (Word Choice), 'more exaggerated, noises' (Subjective Qualifying Adjectives), 'As if the southern states' concerns weren't enough' (Spin/editorializing), 'Hindu nationalist...ideological backbone' (Word Choice/Slant).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: For the one correctly identified instance ('aggressive family welfare programmes'), Word Choice is an appropriate label. The other two items are attribution violations so their type labels are moot, though the labels themselves would have been debatable even if the text were author prose.
